content: We can reframe human active learning's integration of episodic memory and working memory as a biological equivalent of the 2025 ICM curiosity mechanism for autonomous agents, where novel episodic memories act as the intrinsic reward signal that drives active exploration of new content to be held and processed in working memory. This design creates a testable hypothesis that human active learning curiosity scales with the mismatch between new episodic memory content and existing working memory models of the world, just as ICM rewards scale with prediction error between the agent's learned forward model and novel state transitions.
source_topics: Episodic memory integration with working memory for active learning
trigger_topic: Episodic memory integration with working memory for active learning
